title: zsh does not word-split unquoted parameter expansions the way bash does
rule: In zsh, an unquoted scalar expansion like $VAR or `for x in $VAR` is NOT split on whitespace into separate words the way bash and POSIX sh do, so a space-separated string reaches a command as a single argument. Force splitting with ${=VAR}, use a real array (arr=(a b); cmd "${arr[@]}"), or set the SH_WORD_SPLIT option — do not rely on a bash script's implicit splitting under zsh.
triggers: a space-separated variable is passed to a command as one argument in zsh
for x in $VAR runs once with the whole string instead of per word in zsh
File name too long when looping over a variable holding several paths in zsh
a shell script works under bash but breaks under zsh
unquoted variable is not splitting on spaces in zsh
